subject: Biology
title: The Calvin Cycle
description: A quick win: understand The Calvin Cycle.

hook.text: The Calvin cycle doesn't need light directly. It uses the energy captured by light reactions.

buildup.text: CO₂ from the air enters the leaf through tiny pores called stomata.

discovery.text: An enzyme called RuBisCO grabs CO₂ and attaches it to a sugar, starting the building process.

twist.text: RuBisCO is the most abundant protein on Earth—because every plant needs it nonstop.

climax.text: The cycle turns CO₂ into glucose, the sugar that fuels the plant and feeds the food chain.

punchline.text: Air becomes sugar. That's the Calvin cycle.

quiz.question: What is RuBisCO?
options: An enzyme that fixes CO₂ into sugar
